items.headline: Arteta praises 'brave' officials after ruling out West Ham equaliser for 'clear foul'
items.description: Mikel Arteta praised referee Chris Kavanagh and the VAR officials for their "brave" decision to disallow Callum Wilson's equaliser for a "clear foul" in the final seconds of Arsenal's contentious 1-0...

items.headline: Orioles infield coach Miguel Cairo ejected in the 3rd inning against the Athletics
items.description:
BALTIMORE (AP) — Orioles infield coach Miguel Cairo was ejected in the third inning of Sunday's game against the Athletics.
Baltimore manager Craig Albernaz came out to argue with plate umpire Carlos Torres — apparently over a challenge that wasn't granted — with the A's batting in the third. Albernaz eventually went back to the dugout, but around the time he got there, Torres ejected Cairo.

items.headline: Knicks’ OG Anunoby out again for Game 4 against 76ers with hamstring strain, is listed as day to day
items.description:
PHILADELPHIA (AP) — OG Anunoby was ruled out again for Game 4 on Sunday with a strained right hamstring, and the New York Knicks forward remained day to day in the second-round series against the Philadelphia 76ers.
Anunoby was injured late in Game 2 of the Eastern Conference semifinals, in which he scored 24 points.

items.headline: Como secure historic European qualification
items.description: Como made history on Sunday by qualifying for Europe for the first time ever with a 1-0 win at relegated Verona, as a stunning stoppage-time comeback gave Roma a 3-2 win at...

items.headline: Red Sox first baseman Willson Contreras leaves game with bruised hand after getting hit by pitch
items.description:
BOSTON (AP) — Red Sox first baseman Willson Contreras left Sunday's game against the Rays at the end of first inning with a bruised right hand after he was hit by a fastball from Tampa Bay’s Nick Martinez.
Contreras shook his hand in pain and stopped halfway between the plate and first. Boston's interim manager Chad Tracy and a trainer came out to check on him.

items.headline: Giants send struggling reliever Ryan Walker to the minors
items.description:
SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — The San Francisco Giants sent struggling reliever Ryan Walker to Triple-A Sacramento on Sunday following his fourth straight outing when he allowed at least one run.
The move with Walker was one of several made before the Giants played a series finale against the Pittsburgh Pirates. Right-hander Gregory Santos also was optioned to Sacramento, while lefty Sam Hentges was activated from his rehabilitation assignment and righty Dylan Smith was called up from Triple-A.
